g.data-narration: Welcome to Assignment Operators in Java. Learn how to assign values to variables using simple and compound operators.
Assignment operators are fundamental constructs that assign values to variables, forming the backbone of variable initialization in Java.
g.text: Fundamental constructs that assign values to variables, enabling
initialization and modification of data in Java programs.

g.data-narration: The simple assignment operator (=) assigns values with right-to-left associativity. The RHS is evaluated first, then assigned to the LHS variable.
Compound operators combine arithmetic with assignment in one step, making code concise and enabling compiler optimization.
g.text: Equivalent to: variable = variable operator expression

g.data-narration: Java also provides bitwise compound operators for bit-level operations: AND, OR, XOR, and shift assignments.
Watch how compound operators efficiently modify values. Each operation updates the variable in place, evaluating it only once.
When assigning between different types, Java handles it automatically for widening conversions, but requires explicit casting for narrowing.
Java supports chained assignment where multiple variables receive the same value. The assignment returns the value, enabling right-to-left chaining.
Understanding the difference between primitive and reference assignment is crucial. Primitives copy values, while objects copy references.
When you assign object references, both variables point to the same object in memory. Changes through one affect the other.
Assignment operators have the lowest precedence in Java, evaluated after arithmetic. They have right-to-left associativity.
g.text: Assignment has LOWEST precedence - evaluated LAST

g.data-narration: Assignment operators are right-associative. In x = y = z = 10, evaluation happens from right to left: z gets 10, then y, then x.
Avoid common mistakes: confusing assignment (=) with comparison (==), forgetting reference sharing, and ignoring narrowing cast data loss.
Let's trace through a complete example demonstrating simple and compound assignments in a Java program.
Key takeaways: Use = for assignment, compound operators for efficiency, understand reference vs value assignment, and remember right-to-left associativity.
g.text: Assignment operators are the foundation of variable manipulation in Java

g.data-narration: Practice these concepts to master assignment operators. They are essential for writing efficient, concise Java code.
g.text: Practice Tip: Write compound operators in their expanded form to verify your understanding!
